title: Eugenia liberiana
taxonomy: Eugenia liberiana is classified in the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as myrtles. This classification places Eugenia liberiana within the order Myrtales, which includes several families of flowering plants. The genus Eugenia is a group of flowering plants that are primarily native to the tropical regions of the Americas and Africa, and are known for their small to medium-sized trees and shrubs. The family Myrtaceae is native to many parts of the world, including Australia, Asia, Africa, and the Americas. Within the family Myrtaceae, Eugenia liberiana is one of the many species that are commonly found in tropical regions.
uses: Eugenia liberiana has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues and fever. Its leaves are sometimes employed to manage digestive problems, while its bark is utilized to address fever and rheumatic conditions. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat snakebites and other venomous bites. The roots of Eugenia liberiana are believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections. Further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and benefits.

side_effects: Eugenia liberiana is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but its side effects are not well-documented.
